title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints · Layer and Runtime Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective bounded process lifecycle and health policy; isolate the first causal fault in pid 1, signals, shutdown, health, restart, and resource constraints and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded process lifecycle and health policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.
Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 640 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.

Inspect the Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace pid 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping, keep Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Docker Pid1 Signal Reaping, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits-g4dcsb". Tailor its runtime-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, keep Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits-g4dcsb
  - competency: docker-cpu-memory-pids-limits
    artifact: runtime-policy.yaml
    decision: cpu memory pids limits
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: remote context crossing a separately authorized daemon boundary
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: restored checksum and application-level consistency query
    case-key: g4dcsb
    change: |
      init: true
      stop_grace_period: 30s
      healthcheck: bounded-readiness-probe
      restart: on-failure:3
      memory: 256m
      pids_limit: 128
    verify: docker inspect training-worker --format json && docker events --since 10m
    failure: Reject an unbounded probe, restart loop as recovery, shell wrapper that swallows signals, or deployment without memory and PID limits.
    repair: Make PID 1 ownership explicit, bound shutdown and health timing, preserve durable work, and test termination plus resource pressure.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits structural check is only one gate. bounded process lifecycle and health policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cpu Memory Pids Limits, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Containers receive safe CPU and memory limits by default.
